mechanism_narrative: A sustained 5-month increase in industrial production signals a robust demand environment that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in robust demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals a supply-side shock that compresses housing market prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in labor market slowdown periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate below 0.1% signals a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3.5% signals a robust demand environment that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in robust demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production above 105 signals a supply-side shock that compresses housing market prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in labor market slowdown periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3.5% and a 2-month increase in industrial production above 105 signals a robust demand environment that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in robust demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
